 {"id":"bd-16zu","title":"Tests: /settings UI + persistence","description":"# Goal
Add automated coverage for `/settings` UI and persistence.

# Scope
- State tests:
  - open/close settings UI
  - change a setting and apply
- Persistence tests:
  - writes correct JSON structure
  - project overrides global merging

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests are deterministic and use temp dirs.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-172np","title":"[PERF-TEST] Comprehensive unit + E2E tests for all performance features","description":"## Overview

Tracking bead for all PERF integration tests and E2E scripts. This bead is complete when all sub-beads pass.

Tests are now split into per-feature sub-beads that can run as soon as their implementation dependencies are ready, instead of waiting for all 7 PERF implementations to complete.

## Sub-beads

| # | Bead | Tests | Dependencies |
|---|------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | PERF-TEST-1 (bd-231ba) | Cache + Incremental (tests 1-2) | PERF-1, PERF-2 |
| 2 | PERF-TEST-2 (bd-1pfh1) | Cache + Budget (tests 3-4) | PERF-1, PERF-4 |
| 3 | PERF-TEST-3 (bd-42ahe) | Memory + Budget (tests 5-6) | PERF-4, PERF-6 |
| 4 | PERF-TEST-4 (bd-2mjm6) | Buffer + Cache (tests 7-8) | PERF-1, PERF-7 |
| 5 | PERF-TEST-E2E (bd-2oz69) | 4 E2E scripts | All sub-beads |

## Test Infrastructure
- All tests use pi.test.perf_event.v1 JSONL schema
- Artifacts written to tests/artifacts/perf/ for CI retention
- Each test emits a summary line: PASS/FAIL with key metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 sub-beads completed
- [ ] 8 integration tests + 4 E2E scripts passing
- [ ] JSONL logging consistent across all sub-beads
- [ ] Clippy clean","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:04.001252789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T02:46:08.438992303Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T02:46:08.438965283Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-beads completed: PERF-TEST-1 (bd-231ba), PERF-TEST-2 (bd-1pfh1), PERF-TEST-3 (bd-42ahe), PERF-TEST-4 (bd-2mjm6), PERF-TEST-E2E (bd-2oz69). 8 integration tests + 4 E2E scripts all passing. JSONL artifacts emitted to tests/artifacts/perf/.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-172np","depends_on_id":"bd-1pfh1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-172np","depends_on_id":"bd-231ba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-172np","depends_on_id":"bd-2mjm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-172np","depends_on_id":"bd-2oz69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-172np","depends_on_id":"bd-42ahe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2339,"issue_id":"bd-172np","author":"codex","text":"Heads-up: dependency bd-2mjm6 is now closed (verified tests + JSONL events + clippy clean), so this bead has one fewer blocker from the PERF-TEST chain.","created_at":"2026-02-14T02:27:23Z"},{"id":2340,"issue_id":"bd-172np","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-14 codex: claiming PERF test rollup now that all sub-beads are closed. Running final verification pass for e2e_tui_perf scenarios + clippy gate, then will close with evidence if green.","created_at":"2026-02-14T02:46:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-174l","title":"Docs: models.md (models.json overrides + custom providers)","description":"# Goal
Create `docs/models.md` documenting `models.json` overrides and custom provider/model definitions.

# Source Material
- Legacy: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/models.md`
- Rust: `src/models.rs`

- File location (`~/.pi/agent/models.json`).
- Supported schema: providers, baseUrl, headers, models list, compat options.
- How errors are surfaced (and via `/reload` once implemented).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

+{"id":"bd-174r3","title":"Preserve existing V2 sidecar when create_v2_sidecar_from_jsonl rebuild fails","description":"Fresh-eyes audit: src/session.rs create_v2_sidecar_from_jsonl() still removes an existing V2 sidecar before a rebuild from JSONL is known to succeed. A malformed JSONL can therefore destroy a previously valid sidecar. Stage rebuilds/swap safely and add regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:08:15.029294321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:08:15.029294321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-17o","title":"RPC tests: replace MockProvider with VCR playback","description":"Goal:
- Remove MockProvider usage in tests/rpc_mode.rs and use VCR playback (real recorded streams) to validate RPC streaming behavior without mocks.

Scope:
- Replace MockProvider with a VCR-backed provider or adapter that replays recorded SSE chunks.
- Add cassette naming conventions for RPC scenarios (e.g., rpc_basic.json, rpc_toolcall.json).
- Validate real event sequences, stop reasons, and error propagation.
- Force VCR playback mode in tests (no network) and assert zero live HTTP calls.
- Emit detailed logs on each test: cassette path, expected events, actual events, and diffs.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for per-test logs and auto-dump on failure.
- Log cassette path, parsed event timeline, and mismatch details (first divergent event + context).

Acceptance Criteria:
- tests/rpc_mode.rs contains no MockProvider/fake stream.
- All RPC tests run in playback mode with recorded cassettes.
- Logs include cassette name/path, parsed event timeline, and assertion diffs on failure.
- CI runs without network and still validates streaming edge cases.

Dependencies:
- Requires VCR infrastructure (bd-1pf).
- Requires recorded cassette(s) (bd-30u or RPC-specific capture).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:22.325926811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:53:38.892338442Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:53:38.892251690Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}

For each: provide exec mocks with realistic stdout/stderr/exit codes, verify correct command construction and output handling.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:17:07.344374596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:00.396511685Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:00.396360914Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17w1","depends_on_id":"bd-1y3m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1su06","title":"PARITY-UX.6: Add markdown.codeBlockIndent config option","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:43:59.096740229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T23:59:28.770537255Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T23:59:28.770433011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","parity"],"comments":[{"id":2822,"issue_id":"bd-1su06","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## PARITY-UX.6: markdown.codeBlockIndent Config Option

### The Gap
pi-mono has a markdown.codeBlockIndent config option (src/core/settings-manager.ts:39-41) that controls the indentation of code blocks in rendered markdown output.

Our Rust config (src/config.rs) does not have this option.

### Implementation Plan
1. Add codeBlockIndent field to MarkdownConfig (or create it) in src/config.rs:
   ```rust
   pub struct MarkdownConfig {
       pub code_block_indent: Option<u8>,  // default: 2 or 4
   }
   ```
2. Thread the value through to the markdown renderer (rich_rust)
3. Apply indentation when rendering code blocks in terminal output

### Priority
P3 — cosmetic config option.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Config option exists and deserializes
- Value affects code block rendering in terminal output
- Default behavior unchanged when option is absent","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:47:46Z"},{"id":2823,"issue_id":"bd-1su06","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. **Config deserialization**: `{"markdown":{"codeBlockIndent":4}}` parses to MarkdownConfig { code_block_indent: Some(4) }
2. **Default value**: Missing config key → default indentation (2 spaces or whatever pi-mono uses)
3. **Zero value**: codeBlockIndent=0 → no indentation applied
4. **Invalid value**: codeBlockIndent=-1 or 100 → error or clamped

### Integration Tests
5. **Rendering effect**: Set codeBlockIndent=4, render markdown with code block, verify output has 4-space indent
6. **Default rendering**: No config → verify default indent matches pi-mono default

### Structured Logging
- Each test logs: config value, input markdown, rendered output, expected indent, actual indent","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:00:04Z"},{"id":2824,"issue_id":"bd-1su06","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Completed: markdown.codeBlockIndent Config Option

### Implementation
- Added `MarkdownSettings` struct to `src/config.rs` with `code_block_indent: Option<u8>`
  - Supports both `codeBlockIndent` (camelCase) and `code_block_indent` (snake_case)
- Added `markdown: Option<MarkdownSettings>` field to `Config` struct
- Added merge logic in `Config::merge()`
- Applied config to glamour `StyleCodeBlock.block.margin` in:
  - `PiApp::new()` (initial construction)
  - `PiApp::apply_theme()` (theme reload)

### Config Example
```json
{
  "markdown": {
    "codeBlockIndent": 4
  }
}
```

### Files Modified
- `src/config.rs`: Added `MarkdownSettings` struct + Config field + merge + 5 tests
- `src/interactive.rs`: Applied config override to glamour style

### Tests (all pass)
1. `markdown_code_block_indent_deserializes` — camelCase key
2. `markdown_code_block_indent_camel_case_alias` — snake_case key
3. `markdown_code_block_indent_absent` — missing = None
4. `markdown_code_block_indent_zero` — zero value
5. `markdown_merge_prefers_other` — merge precedence

### Verification
- `cargo check --lib` ✓
- `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` — 0 errors in my files
- All 5 tests pass","created_at":"2026-02-14T23:59:21Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tyz","title":"Task: Implement Session Hostcall Handler (pi.session)","description":"Objective: Implement runtime handling for pi.session(op,args) hostcalls so extensions can read session state and append data. Background: Session access per EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE.md §12.6 (getMessages/getState/getFile) and FEATURE_PARITY.md (get_state/get_messages/set_name); ext-compat notes include append entry + set name. Supported ops (case-insensitive): get_state, get_messages, get_entries, get_branch, get_file, get_name, set_name, append_message, append_entry. Implementation: dispatch in src/extensions.rs and src/extension_dispatcher.rs; normalize ops and map invalid payloads to invalid_request; derive get_file/get_name from state. Tests: unit tests in src/extension_dispatcher.rs cover get_state/get_file/get_name/set_name, get_messages/entries/branch, append_message, append_entry, unknown op error, invalid append_message error. E2E: JSONL capture/replay tracked in bd-6vcm.","acceptance_criteria":"[x] Session ops dispatched in src/extensions.rs; [x] Session ops dispatched in src/extension_dispatcher.rs; [x] Unit tests listed above pass; [x] Conformance coverage tracked in bd-6vcm; [x] cargo check --all-targets passes; [x] cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes; [x] cargo fmt --check passes","notes":"Implemented session hostcall dispatch and unit coverage; ran cargo fmt/check/clippy and targeted tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:53:54.241337486Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:42:52.154764699Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:42:52.154583942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tyz","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tyz","depends_on_id":"bd-389q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tz","title":"Unit tests: connector dispatcher + policy engine","description":"Background:
- The connector dispatcher is the security boundary; policy enforcement must be correct.

Steps:
- Unit tests for hostcall routing (read/write/exec/http/env).
- Policy mode tests (strict/prompt/permissive) with allow/deny outcomes.
- Audit log tests (ensure allow/deny entries contain correlation IDs and reason).

Logging requirements:
- Tests must assert log content and redaction of secrets.

Acceptance:
- All policy branches covered; logs are deterministic and audited.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

- src/connectors/http.rs: HttpConnector::redact_url_for_log now strips query/fragment + userinfo before emitting URLs in tracing logs.

Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test ✅","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:05:27Z"},{"id":3453,"issue_id":"bd-1tz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed core unit tests for JS hostcall dispatcher policy + audit logging.

- src/extensions.rs: added CaptureLayer + tests covering host_call.start / policy.decision / host_call.end events; verifies call_id/extension_id/reason + params_hash; asserts secrets never appear in logged fields.
- Covers Prompt policy prompting + cache (prompt_user_allow -> prompt_cache_allow), Strict deny path (not_in_default_caps), and allowed tool routing via write/read.
- Existing integration tests in tests/extensions_manifest.rs already cover ExtensionPolicy::evaluate modes + required_capability_for_host_call mapping.

Commits: 12972f7 (http URL log redaction helper+tests), 3e76a31 (hostcall audit-log tests + non-panicking asserts).

Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test ✅
E2E/JSONL artifacts: N/A (unit-test-only bead).","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:53:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1u0c","title":"Workstream: /share + /copy Parity (gist + clipboard)","description":"# Goal
Match legacy `/copy` and `/share` behaviors in interactive mode.

# Legacy Behavior
## `/copy`
- Copies the last assistant message to clipboard.
- If nothing to copy: show an error.

## `/share`
Legacy implementation (`legacy .../interactive-mode.ts`):
1) Ensure `gh` is installed and logged in (`gh auth status`).
2) Export session to a temp HTML file.
3) Show a cancellable loader UI while creating a **secret gist**:
   - `gh gist create --public=false <tmpFile>`
4) Parse gist URL from stdout and extract gist ID.
5) Compute viewer URL via `PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL` (default `https://buildwithpi.ai/session/`) and show:
   - `Share URL: <viewer>#<gistId>`
   - `Gist: <gistUrl>`

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/copy` is feature-gated and currently just reports “clipboard not enabled”.
- `/share` only writes an HTML file to temp and prints the path; no gist.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Implement cross-platform clipboard copy for `/copy` with actionable error messages.
- Implement gist-based `/share` with:
  - `gh` presence/auth checks
  - cancellable UI/loader
  - robust gist ID parsing
  - env var `PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL` support
  - cleanup of temp files

# Testing
- Add tests that validate:
  - gist ID parsing
  - error messaging when `gh` missing or not logged in
  - `/copy` behavior with and without clipboard support (mocking where needed)

# Dependencies
- Keybinding layer is helpful for cancellation UX but not required.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] /copy copies last assistant message to clipboard
[ ] /share creates private gist via gh and prints viewer URL
[ ] PI_SHARE_VIEWER_URL supported
[ ] Share operation is cancellable and cleans up temp files

 {"id":"bd-3sf","title":"Spec: Extension manifest + capability inference","description":"# Goal
Define how extensions declare and how tooling infers **capabilities**.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Manifest schema: name, version, entrypoint, required capabilities (scoped).
- Static inference rules from source (imports, string literals, config files).
- Merge policy: declared ∪ inferred, with explicit user overrides.
- Validation: refuse capabilities not allowed by policy.
- Log schema for capability resolution (what was declared vs inferred).

# Rationale
- Capability inference keeps extensions minimal and reduces permission creep.
- Clear logs prevent confusing "why was this denied" questions.

# Tests
- Unit fixtures for inference with deterministic ordering.
- E2E harness validates capability resolution logs.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
